articles.title: Atomic-level engineering of Cu nanoclusters improves conversion of CO₂ to fuel
articles.summary: Researchers at Tohoku University have discovered a promising strategy that converts harmful carbon dioxide into valuable fuels and chemicals by precisely altering nanoclusters made of copper.

articles.title: New research reveals the hidden pollution left behind by fireworks

articles.title: Chemists capture structure of the elusive borylnitrene trapped in a crystal using X-ray
articles.summary: Nitrenes are the ghosts of synthetic chemistry, formed in an instant and gone just as quickly, rearranging into something entirely different. These highly reactive intermediates are widely used in synthesis, yet remain…

articles.title: Audacious mission to rescue NASA's falling telescope has launched
articles.summary: NASA’s Swift space telescope is reaching the end of its two-decade run in orbit – unless a satellite launched on 3 July can give it a lifesaving boost

articles.title: Groundbreaking Swift spacecraft rescue mission launches

articles.title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies

articles.title: 1 private spacecraft intercepts another on Space Force's groundbreaking 'Victus Haze' mission
articles.summary: A U.S. Space Force mission pitting two satellites against each other has completed the organization's first tactical intercept of an orbital target.

articles.title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star
articles.summary: "It's like using a time machine to peer into the distant future of our solar system."

articles.title: Ancient bees turned tooth sockets into tiny nurseries 20,000 years ago
articles.summary: A stunning fossil discovery shows that ancient bees used the empty tooth sockets of mammal bones as tiny nests after owls scattered the bones across a cave floor 20,000 years ago. It's the first known evidence of bees…

articles.title: Ancient hobbit-like humans may have survived on meat left behind by Komodo dragons

articles.title: Human brains may have got bigger for no particular reason
articles.summary: Our brains are large compared with other animals, so it is tempting to assume there was an evolutionary advantage to them – but that may not be true at all

articles.title: Why Antarctica froze millions of years before the Arctic

articles.title: Traces of Earth's primordial magma ocean discovered in lava from a modern volcanic eruption

articles.title: Collapse of AMOC ocean current may already be locked in
articles.summary: The fate of the Atlantic Ocean current that keeps Europe’s climate warm depends on our carbon emissions and the rate of ice melt from Greenland, but there is a chance that a shutdown is already inevitable

articles.title: A volcano has erupted remnants of Earth's primordial magma ocean
articles.summary: Earth was once covered by a global magma ocean, which later cooled and crystallised – now traces of this primordial event have been found in magma from a young volcano in the Indian Ocean

articles.title: Scientists discover smart way to supercharge soft robotics and better support rehabilitation patients
articles.summary: Researchers have found an ingenious way to make soft robots and wearable technology more than three times more powerful by harnessing the surface tension of a tiny liquid metal droplet smaller than a raindrop.

articles.title: Tiny magnetic waves could unlock quantum computers the size of a penny

articles.title: Universities must rethink how they prepare students for an AI-powered world, study argues

articles.title: Can the biggest problems in AI be solved by philosophy?
articles.summary: AI companies are hiring philosophy graduates to help them understand the nature of consciousness, whether it can be replicated and how their systems can be made better and more reliable

articles.title: US government wants to have a useful quantum computer by 2028
articles.summary: The US government is trying to speed up the development of quantum computers so it can have one sooner

articles.title: The weirdness of neutrinos could completely rewrite particle physics
articles.summary: A philosopher has put forward an argument for rethinking how particles are defined within the standard model of particle physics

articles.title: June heatwave may have killed around 20,000 people in Europe
articles.summary: It will be some months before the true toll of Europe's worst-ever heatwave is confirmed, but researchers can estimate a death count based on how many people died in Europe during previous hot periods

articles.title: Random wobbles in time could finally solve gravity’s greatest mystery
articles.summary: The question of how gravity interacts with the quantum world has long perplexed physicists, but a non-quantum theory of space-time could present an answer

articles.title: Geoengineering could expose plane passengers to sulphuric acid
articles.summary: A proposed technique to counter global warming by spraying sun-reflecting particles near the poles would cause commercial flights to pass through clouds of sulphuric acid, posing a danger to passengers and crew
